output:
## Analysis

Given:
- P(visited England) = 49%, so P(not visited England) = 51%
- P(not visited England ∧ fired) = 14%
- P(visited England ∧ fired) = 37%

**Conditional probabilities:**

- P(fired | not visited England) = 0.14 / 0.51 ≈ **27.45%**
- P(fired | visited England) = 0.37 / 0.49 ≈ **75.51%**

**No.** The chance of the employee being fired is *larger* when observing having visited England (≈75.5%) compared to not having visited England (≈27.5%).
